verdict.label: Go bodyboarding
verdict.headline: Go bodyboarding: Belgas Mid-morning — 11:00
verdict.reason: Best combined score from the checked sources: Rare window session, High confidence, with no major blocker for Belgas.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Belgas looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, High confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 125 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 60/100, and glassy conditions.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at mid tide also works on Belgas, but low tide is not a deal-breaker, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Expect: scan from the dunes first because banks and channels shift fast.
best.shortExplanation: Belgas is an open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything · label Rare window, High confidence · 0.6-0.9 m · 125 kJ · 11 s period · 10-13s at mid tide also works on Belgas, but low tide is not a deal-breaker · glassy conditions.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a clean fun-sized window for Fun progression, High confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 77 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 61/100, and glassy conditions.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 10-13s at mid tide suits the reef shelves and gives long workable walls.  Expect: it can get busy, and it is usually more flow than heavy barrel.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is a dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides · label Fun progression, High confidence · 0.6-0.9 m · 77 kJ · 11 s period · 10-13s at mid tide suits the reef shelves and gives long workable walls · glassy conditions.

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 70 kJ, 13 s period, workable consistency 59/100, and glassy conditions.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 10-13s at mid tide works well on exposed beaches, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Expect: banks, rips and wind exposure decide whether it is magic or messy.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is an exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal · label Rare window, Medium confidence · 0.3-0.6 m · 70 kJ · 13 s period · 10-13s at mid tide works well on exposed beaches · glassy conditions.

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean fun-sized window for Heavy / advanced, High confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 53 kJ, 11 s period, lully 25/100, and glassy conditions.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort; 10-13s at mid tide is the prime barrel / wedge setup for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; Supertubos is a check-first bodyboard size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Supertubos is a fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly · label Heavy / advanced, High confidence · 0.6-1.1 m · 53 kJ · 11 s period · 10-13s at mid tide is the prime barrel / wedge setup for Supertubos / Molhe Leste · glassy conditions · Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; Supertubos is a check-first bodyboard size.
warnings: Supertubos is a check-first bodyboard size
